coredata.dc:title: A highly sensitive colorimetric probe for Cd2+, Hg2+ and ascorbic acid determination based on trithiocyanuric acid-AuNPs
coredata.dc:creator: Wang, Jing
Fang, Xian
Cui, Xiaoqing
Zhang, Yuhua
Zhao, Hong
Li, Xiangjun
He, Yujian
coredata.dc:description: A highly sensitive and selective colorimetric assay is proposed for the detection of mercury ions (Hg2+), cadmium ions (Cd2+) and ascorbic acid (AA) using trithiocyanuric acid (TMT) functionalized gold nanoparticles (TMT-AuNPs). TMT-AuNPs are dispersed in 40 mM NaCl solution, while the presence of Hg2+ and Cd2+ can induce TMT-AuNPs aggregate due to the strong interaction of Hg2+ and Cd2+ with TMT. Then the quantitative detection of Hg2+ and Cd2+can be realized in the linear range from 5 × 10−9 to 1 × 10−6 M and 1 × 10−8 to 3 × 10−7 M, with a lower detection limit of 2.8 nM for Hg2+ and 3.5 nM for Cd2+ (S/N = 3), respectively. To distinguish Hg2+ from Cd2+, a reductive biological small molecule ascorbic acid (AA) was used based on the different redox interaction of AA with Hg2+ and Cd2+. When the mixture of AA and Hg2+ was added into TMT-AuNPs solution, the state of TMT-AuNPs was still dispersed because Hg2+ was reduced by AA. On the contrary, when the mixture of AA and Cd2+ was added into TMT-AuNPs solution, the TMT-AuNPs was aggregated and the color of the solution has no obvious change compared to the system of Cd2+-TMT-AuNPs since the weak interaction between AA and Cd2+. Thus, AA could be detected. A linear correlation exists between the ratio of A640/A520 and the concentration of AA in the range from 1 × 10−8 to 1 × 10−6 M with a detection limit of 4.8 nM (S/N = 3). Importantly, it has been successfully applied to determine Hg2+, Cd2+ in lake water and AA in vitamins C tablet.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Colorimetric detection
Trithiocyanuric acid
Mercury ions
Cadmium ions
Ascorbic acid

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Colorimetric detection of Cd2+ and Hg2+
Effect of the concentration of NaCl and TMT
Effect of Nano-Gold with different morphologies to detect Cd2+ and Hg2+
Sensitive colorimetric determination of Hg2+ and Cd2+

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: UV–vis spectra of (a) original AuNPs solution, (b) TMT-AuNPs, (c) the TMT-AuNPs solution containing 500 nM Hg2+ and (d) the TMT-AuNPs solution containing 300 nM Cd2+; the inset photographic image is the corresponding colorimetric response.
TEM images of AuNPs in the presence of 300 nM Cd2+ (a) and 500 nM Hg2+ (b).

Photographs, absorption spectra, and plots of A640/A520 versus the concentration (inset) in 10 mM PBS (pH 7.0) containing TMT-AuNPs, and 40 mM NaCl upon addition of (a) 0–300 nM Cd2+ and (b) 0–5000 nM Hg2+. The incubation time was 10 min. The error bars represented standard deviations based on three independent measurements.

(a) The corresponding color change photo images of TMT-AuNPs added with different metal ions (0.3 μM Cd2+, Hg2+ 0.5 μM, and 1 μM other metal ions). A640/A520 upon the addition of Cd2+ (b), Hg2+ (c) and selected ions to TMT-AuNPs sensing assay. Black bars represent the addition of single interferential ions (1 μM) and red bars represent the addition of Cd2+ (0.3 μM) or Hg2+ (0.5 μM) in the presence of 1 μM interferential ions (X).

Photographs of (a) different concentration of Hg2+ (a–d: 0.5, 0.8, 1.0, 5.0 μM) and Hg2+ with AA (a′–d′) added into TMT-AuNPs in the presence of 40 mM NaCl; (b) different concentration of Cd2+ (e–h) and Cd2+ with AA (e′–h′) added into TMT-AuNPs in the presence of 40 mM NaCl. (c) A640/A520 upon the addition of different concentration of Hg2+ and 1.0 μM AA to TMT-AuNPs sensing assay. Black bars represent the addition of single Hg2+ ions and red bars are the addition of Hg2+ in the presence of AA (1 μM). TEM images of AuNPs in the presence of 2.5 μM TMT and 1.0 μM Hg2+ (d) and in the presence of 2.5 μM TMT, 1.0 μM Hg2+ and 1 μM AA (e).

(a) Photographs, absorption spectra, and plots of A640/A520 versus the concentration (inset) in 10 mM PBS (pH 7.0) containing TMT-AuNPs, 1 μM Hg2+, and 40 mM NaCl upon addition of 0–10 μM AA. The incubation time was 10 min (b) UV–vis absorption ratio of A640/A520 the TMT-AuNPs in the presence of AA (1 μM) and other interferents (10 μM). The error bars represented standard deviations based on three independent measurements.
Schematic illustration of the colorimetric detection of Cd2+, Hg2+ and AA.
Comparison of this work with some methods for Cd2+ detection.

ce:legend.ce:simple-para: GSH: glutathione; DP-AuNPs: di-(1H-pyrrol-2-yl)methanethione functionalized gold nanoparticles; R4: Thiophene-2-carboxylic acid isoquinolin-1-ylmethylene-hydrazide; PTBO-Au: poly (toluidine blue O)-gold; TMT-AuNPs: trithiocyanuric acid-AuNPs.

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Comparison of this work with some methods for Hg2+ detection.
Recovery of Cd2+ and Hg2+ in lake water (n = 3).
Comparison of this work with other methods for AA detection.
Recovery of standard addition to detect AA in vitamins C tablet (n = 3).

head.ce:title: A highly sensitive colorimetric probe for Cd2+, Hg2+ and ascorbic acid determination based on trithiocyanuric acid-AuNPs

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Chemical Sciences, University of Chinese Academy of Sciences, 19A YuQuan Road, Beijing 100049, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: School of Chemical Sciences, University of Chinese Academy of Sciences

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: A highly sensitive and selective colorimetric assay is proposed for the detection of mercury ions (Hg2+), cadmium ions (Cd2+) and ascorbic acid (AA) using trithiocyanuric acid (TMT) functionalized gold nanoparticles (TMT-AuNPs). TMT-AuNPs are dispersed in 40 mM NaCl solution, while the presence of Hg2+ and Cd2+ can induce TMT-AuNPs aggregate due to the strong interaction of Hg2+ and Cd2+ with TMT. Then the quantitative detection of Hg2+ and Cd2+can be realized in the linear range from 5 × 10−9 to 1 × 10−6 M and 1 × 10−8 to 3 × 10−7 M, with a lower detection limit of 2.8 nM for Hg2+ and 3.5 nM for Cd2+ (S/N = 3), respectively. To distinguish Hg2+ from Cd2+, a reductive biological small molecule ascorbic acid (AA) was used based on the different redox interaction of AA with Hg2+ and Cd2+. When the mixture of AA and Hg2+ was added into TMT-AuNPs solution, the state of TMT-AuNPs was still dispersed because Hg2+ was reduced by AA. On the contrary, when the mixture of AA and Cd2+ was added into TMT-AuNPs solution, the TMT-AuNPs was aggregated and the color of the solution has no obvious change compared to the system of Cd2+-TMT-AuNPs since the weak interaction between AA and Cd2+. Thus, AA could be detected. A linear correlation exists between the ratio of A640/A520 and the concentration of AA in the range from 1 × 10−8 to 1 × 10−6 M with a detection limit of 4.8 nM (S/N = 3). Importantly, it has been successfully applied to determine Hg2+, Cd2+ in lake water and AA in vitamins C tablet.

In 40 mM NaCl solution, a highly sensitive and selective colorimetric detection of mercury ions, cadmium ions, and ascorbic acid is realized based on trithiocyanuric acid (TMT) functionalized gold nanoparticles.
fx1

ce:section.ce:para: Contamination of water by heavy metal ions (e.g., mercury, cadmium, silver and lead) can cause serious environmental and health problems due to acute and/or chronic toxicity to biological organisms [1,2]. Mercury ions (Hg2+) and cadmium ions (Cd2+) are two of the toxic heavy metal pollutants which wildly exist in water, soil and even in food. Mercury exposure has serious adverse effects on the brain, kidneys, and nervous and endocrine systems in humans and animals [3]. Moreover, cadmium ion is also considered as a highly toxic heavy metal ion which may have several chronic effects on humans such as cancer, renal dysfunction, hypercalciuria, and enhanced tumor growth [4,5]. To date, many different techniques were adopted for detecting Hg2+ and Cd2+, including atomic absorption spectrometry (AAS) [6], atomic emission spectrometry (AES) [7], inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry (ICP-MS) [8], electrochemical method [9], high-performance liquid chromatography [10], fluorescence assay [11] and colorimetric methods [12–14]. Among them, colorimetric methods are extremely attractive because they are simple, rapid, cheap and the detection results can be easily read out with the naked eyes.

Gold nanoparticles (AuNPs) have attracted much attention in colorimetric detection because of their unique and size dependent optical and electronic properties. One of the most important characteristics of AuNPs is their localized surface plasmon resonance (LSPR) which refers to the collective oscillation of the conducting electrons of AuNPs when their frequency matches that of the incident electromagnetic radiation [15,16]. LSPR of the AuNPs is mainly related to the size, shape, composition, interparticle distance, and dielectric constant (refractive-index) of the surrounding medium [17,18]. Colorimetric methods based on gold nanoparticles have been used to detect metallic ions [19–21]. For example, colorimetric detection of Hg2+ was reported based on the interaction between Hg2+ and the hydrosulfide group (–SH) [22] or pyridine's nitrogen atom [23]. On the other hand, di-(1H-pyrrol-2-yl)methanethione functionalized gold nanoparticles was used to detect Cd2+ [24]. However, the detection limits of few colorimetric reports fit in with the Maximum Contaminate Level (MCL) of mercury (2 µg L−1) and cadmium (5 µg L−1) in drinking water permitted by the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) [25]. Thus it is urgent to improve the sensitivity and selectivity of colorimetric detection of Hg2+ and Cd2+.

As we know, high concentration of NaCl plays an important role in the sensitive colorimetric determination of Hg2+ and Cd2+. However, bare AuNPs can easily aggregate in high concentration of NaCl. To prevent the aggregation of AuNPs, proper stabilizer of AuNPs is necessary. Because of sulfhydryl group and heterocyclic N atoms of trithiocyanuric acid (TMT), TMT can bind strongly to the AuNPs. Thus we modified AuNPs with TMT to protect AuNPs from aggregation in 40 mM NaCl solution ( Scheme 1 a). While in the presence of Cd2+ or Hg2+, the interaction between TMT and Cd2+ or Hg2+ decreased the interparticle distance and inducing aggregation of AuNPs. Therefore, the colorimetric detection of Hg2+ and Cd2+ was sensitively realized.

But unfortunately, it was difficult to discriminate Hg2+ from Cd2+. Among the existing reports about colorimetric method for Hg2+ and Cd2+, most of them can just detect only one metal ion, and even though a few probes are applied to detect Hg2+ and Cd2+ at the same time [26]. For Hg2+ and Cd2+, they are two elements in the same group of the periodic table with a closed-shell d10 electronic configuration, and usually show similar chemical behavior [5]. Some fluorescent chemosensors have been designed and used to selectively recognize and sense Hg2+ and Cd2+. But these sensors are generally derivatives of fluorescent dyes, and the methods of preparation are complex and usually harmful to the environment [27,28]. Therefore, it is highly desirable to find a simple and sensitive green reagent to selectively discriminate Hg2+ and Cd2+. As well known, ascorbic acid (AA) is a reductive biological small molecule, which plays an important role in biological system. Inspired by the different interaction of AA with Hg2+ and Cd2+, this problem was successfully solved by the addition of AA. That is to say, AA can reduce Hg2+ to Hg [29], hence the state of TMT-AuNPs did not change in the presence of the mixture of AA and Hg2+ (Scheme 1 b). While TMT-AuNPs were highly aggregated in the presence of the mixture of AA and Cd2+ owing to the weak interaction between AA and Cd2+ (Scheme 1 b). Furthermore, the color of the TMT-AuNPs solution containing Hg2+ would progress from grey to red with the increase of the concentration of AA, thus AA can be quantitatively determined. Particularly, the proposed method was successfully applied to determine Hg2+, Cd2+ and AA in real samples.

All chemicals used were of analytical reagent grade without further purification and solutions were prepared with Milli-Q-purified distilled water. Hydrogen tetrachloroaurate hydrate (HAuCl4·4H2O) was bought from Sinopharm Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd. (Beijing, China). Trithiocyanuric acid (TMT) was purchased from J&K Chemicals (Beijing, China). Trisodium citrate, sodium chloride and all of the metal salts used in this study were purchased from the Beijing Chemical Reagent Company (Beijing, China). Dopamine (DA, 99%) was purchased from Acros Organics (New Jersey, USA). Ascorbic acid (AA, 99.7%) was obtained from Shantou Xilong Chemical Factory (Guangdong, China). Uric acid (UA, 99%) was bought from Alfa Aesar (England). Other amino acids were provided from Alfa Aesar (England).

The UV–Vis absorbance spectra were recorded with a UV-2550 spectrophotometer at ambient temperature (20 ± 2 ℃) with Quartz cuvettes. Transmission electron microscopy (TEM) images were obtained by using a Philips EM-400. All of the pH measurements were performed with a PB-10 pH meter (Sartorius, Germany).

The AuNPs were prepared by a citrate-mediated reduction of HAuCl4 [30]. All glassware is cleaned in aqua regia (HCl:HNO3 = 3:1) and rinsed with ultrapure H2O prior to use. The solution of HAuCl4 (1 mM, 150 mL) was placed in a round-bottom flask with a reflux condenser and boiled by oil bath whilst stirring. Then 38.8 mM trisodium citrate (15 mL) was added quickly to the solution, which was heated for another 15 min, during which time its color changed from pale yellow to wine red. The solution was cooled to room temperature with continuous stirring and then was stored in a refrigerator at 4 °C. The citrate-stabilized suspension of AuNPs showed an absorption peak at 520 nm, which was ascribed to the surface plasmon of the 13 nm AuNPs. The concentration of the AuNPs solution as determined by UV–vis spectrometry was 10 nM [31].

ce:section.ce:section-title: Colorimetric detection of Cd2+ and Hg2+
ce:section.ce:para: During the procedure of the detection of Cd2+ and Hg2+, AuNPs and the TMT solution are mixed with phosphate buffer. After incubating for about 30 s at room temperature, AuNPs functionalized with TMT (TMT-AuNPs) were obtained, and then the NaCl solution and different concentrations of Cd2+ or Hg2+ are added to the solution, respectively. After thoroughly shaking and incubating for about 10 min, the color changes of the solutions and their corresponding UV–vis absorption spectra are recorded.

Different concentrations of AA were mixed with Hg2+ solution for 10 min. After that, TMT-AuNPs and NaCl solution were added into the solution and the mixture was diluted to 2 mL with phosphate buffer. Finally, the UV–vis absorption spectra of the solutions were measured over the wavelength range from 400 nm to 800 nm. For pharmaceutical analysis, vitamins C tablets was weighed and powdered in a mortar. 0.06 g powder was dissolved in 20 mL ultrapure water and centrifuged at 9000 rpm for 10 min, and then insoluble components were removed from solution. The solution filtrated twice with 0.22 µm Millipore filter and then stored in dark at 4 °C. The filtrate was diluted 100-fold with ultrapure water before analysis [32].

It is well known that AuNPs easily aggregate in high concentration of NaCl when there is no other stabilizing agent on the surface of AuNPs [33]. To protect AuNPs from aggregation, they were functionalized with TMT. The spectra of the UV–vis absorption is shown in Fig. 1 , a characteristic SPR band of AuNPs is observed in the spectrum at approximate 520 nm and the solution color is ruby red (inset of Fig. 1). In the absence of Hg2+ or Cd2+, the addition of TMT (2.5 μM) to the AuNPs slightly induces a red shift of AuNPs maximal absorption band. However, in the presence of Hg2+ (500 nM) or Cd2+ (300 nM) the absorbance of AuNPs at 520 nm decreased gradually and the absorbance at 640 nm correspondingly increased and the color changes from red to blue due to the interaction between TMT and Hg2+ or Cd2+ [34]. TEM images of AuNPs further prove that AuNPs indeed aggregated after the addition of Cd2+ and Hg2+ ( Fig. 2), and the aggregation induced by Cd2+ (Fig. 2 a) is obviously stronger than that by Hg2+ (Fig. 2 b). However, the TMT-AuNPs probe cannot distinguish them when the high concentration of Hg2+ ions coexist with Cd2+ ions, because they can similarly cause the aggregation of TMT-AuNPs and the color of solution changed from red to light grey. To address this problem, we used a simple redox reaction between Hg2+ and AA. In the presence of AA, Hg2+ was reduced to Hg [29], thus the aggregation of TMT-AuNPs induced by Hg2+ was prevented. Meanwhile, with the increase of the concentration of AA, the color of the solution changes from grey to red. On the contrary, in the presence of AA, the aggregation of TMT-AuNPs induced by Cd2+ was still observed, and the color of the solution had no obvious change compared to the system of Cd2+-TMT-AuNPs. Therefore, the selective colorimetric detection of Hg2+, Cd2+ and AA was realized.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Effect of the concentration of NaCl and TMT
ce:section.ce:para: To obtain good sensitivity, we first study the salt effect on the stability of the AuNPs. With the increase of the concentration of NaCl, the absorbance of AuNPs at 520 nm decreased gradually and the absorbance at 640 nm correspondingly increased. We used the ratio of the absorbance of AuNPs at 640 and 520 nm (A640/A520) to evaluate the degree of AuNPs aggregation. A lower A640/A520 indicates that AuNPs disperse well in the solution, and a higher A640/A520 is related with the aggregated AuNPs [35]. When the concentration of NaCl was 40 mM, A640/A520 was up to the highest, which indicated that AuNPs aggregated almost completely (Fig. S1 a). Therefore, 40 mM NaCl was used to the detection system for the following experiments.

To obtain the optimum concentration of TMT for stabilizing AuNPs, we investigated the colorimetric response of AuNPs in the presence of different concentrations of TMT. With the increase of the concentrations of TMT, the color of AuNPs containing 40 mM NaCl changed from blue to red, which indicated that TMT-AuNPs were well dispersed. Transmission electron microscopy (TEM) images verified this process (Fig. S1 c, d and e). The value of A640/A520 decreased, and it reached the minimum when the concentration of TMT was 2.5 μM (Fig. S1 b). Therefore, the concentration of 2.5 μM was selected throughout the experiments.

The pH value of the system also played an important part in the sensing process. We prepared a series of 0.01 M phosphate buffer, with pH values of 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9 and 10, respectively, to estimate the effect of pH on the aggregation of TMT-AuNPs with 40 mM NaCl. As shown in Fig. S2, the minimum A640/A520 was obtained at pH 7. Therefore, pH 7 was chose in throughout the experiments.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Effect of Nano-Gold with different morphologies to detect Cd2+ and Hg2+
ce:section.ce:para: We have synthesized Au nanorods [36] and Au nanoplates [37] according to the methods in the literature. The UV–vis spectra of Au nanoparticles, Au nanorods and Au nanoplates were shown in Fig. S3 a. Under the optimum conditions, Cd2+ or Hg2+ was added into the solutions of Au nanoparticles (Fig. S3 b), Au nanorods ( Fig. S3 c) and Au nanoplates (Fig. S3 d). Only gold nanoparticles aggregated in the presence of Cd2+ or Hg2+, and the color and UV–vis absorption of the solution changed obviously, but there was no significant change in Au nanorods and Au nanoplates. Therefore, the gold nanoparticles are applied to detect Cd2+ and Hg2+.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Sensitive colorimetric determination of Hg2+ and Cd2+
ce:section.ce:para: Under the optimum conditions, the sensitivity of the system was evaluated towards Hg2+ and Cd2+. As the increasing of the concentration of Cd2+, the UV–vis absorbance curves shows red-shift and broaden gradually, and a peak emerges slowly at longer wavelength. The ratio of A640/A520 as a function of the concentration of Cd2+ was plotted in Fig. 3 a, which increased gradually with the increase of the concentration of Cd2+, and the color changed from red to grey. The detection limit of this method was as low as 3.5 nM (S/N = 3), and a good linear relationship (correlation coefficient R2 = 0.9925) between A640/A520 and concentration of Cd2+ was observed in the range of 10–300 nM. Similarly, the photograph of the sensing solutions with Hg2+ concentration varying between 0 and 5 μM is shown in Fig. 3 b. A linear correlation existed between the ratio of A640/A520 and the concentration of Hg2+ in the range of 0.005–1 μM (R2 = 0.9929). The detection limit for Hg2+ was 2.8 nM (S/N = 3). In addition, the results obtained by the proposed method were compared with those obtained by other methods. Table 1 and Table 2 shows that the proposed method exhibited wider linear range and lower detection limit.

To inspect the selectivity of the colorimetric method towards Hg2+ and Cd2+, several cations and anions were employed to test the selectivity for the determination of the Hg2+ and Cd2+. Fig. 4 a shows that 1 μM other ions including Li+, K+, Mg2+, Ca2+, Cr3+, Mn2+, Fe2+, Co2+, Ni2+, Cu2+, Zn2+, Cd2+, Al3+, Ce3+, HCO3 − and AC− alone had no obvious effect on the color of the TMT-AuNPs. However, as the addition of Hg2+ (0.5 μM) or Cd2+ (0.3 μM) alone, the color of AuNPs changed from red to grey and the absorbance ratio A640/A520 obviously increased compared to others. Moreover, competitive experiments were performed in the presence of Hg2+ (0.5 μM) and Cd2+ (0.3 μM) respectively with other ions (X) above-mentioned. The value of A640/A520 from the mixtures of Hg2+ (0.5 μM) or Cd2+ (0.3 μM) with X changed similarly with that from Hg2+ (0.5 μM) or Cd2+ (0.3 μM) alone respectively, which indicated that the sensing system possessed excellent selectivity for Hg2+ and Cd2+ ions (Fig. 4 b and c).

In order to validate the reliability of the proposed method, real environmental samples (lake water collected from Yanqi Lake) had been analyzed and the results is shown in Table 3. The recovery percentages at different concentration of Cd2+ and Hg2+ in the experiments were desirable, which indicated the potential practical application of the colorimetric assay for real sample detections [44].

To investigate the difference of AA interacted with Hg2+ and Cd2+, the AA was mixed with the different concentration of Hg2+ and Cd2+ respectively for 10 min, and the mixture solutions were added into the TMT-AuNPs and NaCl solution. As shown in Fig. 5 a, in the absence of AA, the high concentration of Hg2+ ions promotes the aggregation of AuNPs and the solution becomes dark grey. While 1 μM of AA kept AuNPs dispersed and red in color because Hg2+ was reduced by AA. TEM images verify the results (Fig. 5 d and e). When the content of Hg2+ was up to 5 μM, 1 μM of AA can’t prevent AuNPs aggregated and dark grey in color because of the low content of AA. However, whether AA was mixed with Cd2+ or not, the TMT-AuNPs retained aggregated and the color of solution had no noticeable changed (Fig. 5 b). Therefore, this process can discriminate Hg2+ and Cd2+ and detect AA. In addition, we further explored the interaction between Hg2+, AA and TMT-AuNPs. As shown in Fig. S4 , Hg2+ ions (1 μM) promoted the aggregation of TMT-AuNPs and the solution became dark grey (Fig. S4 a). When AA (10 μM) was firstly reacted with Hg2+ solution for 10 min and then their mixture was added into TMT-AuNPs, the color of the solution was red (Fig. S4 b). However, when AA was directly added to the solution of TMT-AuNPs in the presence of Hg2+, the color of the solution was still grey (Fig. S4 c). Thus, we thought that AA and Hg2+ was not probably competitive binding with TMT-AuNPs. It might be the reduction of Hg2+ by AA and the decrease of the concentration of free Hg2+ in the solution. Thus, the color of solution changed from grey to red.

In order to make sure of the completely reaction between AA and Hg2+, we optimized the concentration of Hg2+ for this colorimetric assay. When Hg2+ was alone added to the TMT-AuNPs solution, the value of A640/A520 increased with the increase of the concentration of Hg2+ in the range from 0.5 to 5.0 μM. When the mixture of Hg2+ and AA was added, the ratio of A640/A520 was declined compared with the same concentration of Hg2+, since the concentration of free Hg2+ in the solution was declined due to its reduction by AA. However, AA cannot completely reduce Hg2+ in the solution when the concentration of Hg2+ was larger than 1 μM, which induced the residue of Hg2+ in the solution and the ratio of A640/A520 increased (Fig. 5 c). Thus, 1 μM Hg2+ was chose in the next experiments.

The linear range of the sensing assay was examined with different concentrations of AA under the above-mentioned optimized conditions. When the concentration of AA increased from 0 to 10 μM, the absorption peak at 520 nm increased while that at 640 nm decreased, and induced the value of A640/A520 of the system decreasing gradually, along with the color of the solutions changing from grey to red gradually ( Fig. 6 a). A linear correlation existed between the ratio of A640/A520 and the concentration of AA in the range of 0.01–1 μM (R2 = 0.9979). The detection limit for Hg2+ was 4.8 nM (S/N = 3). Table 4 shows that the linear range and the detection limit are lower than those recently reported by other sensors of AA detection.

Besides, we had also studied the specificity of the system as an assay approach toward AA (1 μM) against other species (10 μM), e.g., histidine (His), phenylalanine (Phe), glucose (Glu), DA, UA, cysteine (Cys) and tyrosine (Tyr). Fig. 6 b shows that except for AA, all other species induced significant aggregation of AuNPs in the presence of Hg2+. This observation indicated favorable selectivity for the assay of AA in the presence of the other species. In addition, this approach was applied to AA detection in vitamins C tablet. As indicated in Table 5, the values found for these samples were identical with the expected values, which validated the reliability and practicality of this strategy.

In conclusion, we developed a novel facile and rapid method for Hg2+, Cd2+ and AA detection using TMT-AuNPs. The proposed assay shows high selectivity and sensitivity for the detection of Hg2+ and Cd2+. Moreover, the presence of AA can distinguish Hg2+ from Cd2+, and quantitative detection of AA can be also realized. Compared with traditional AuNPs-based colorimetric detection methods, our methods are easy, low-cost and feasible, showing good potential for the visual detection of Hg2+, Cd2+ and AA in real samples.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was supported by a grant from the National Natural Science Foundation of China (Grant nos. 21676270 and 21205132), Beijing Natural Science Foundation (Grant no. 2182083) and the National Key Research and Development Plan (Grant no. 2016YFF0203700).

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data associated with this article can be found in the online version at doi:10.1016/j.talanta.2018.05.084.
